summary.narrative: Whitmer entered office with ambitious 2018 platform including road infrastructure, education access, and workforce development. Mixed delivery: achieved tuition-free community college, childcare expansion, abortion protection, right-to-work repeal, EV investment attraction. Major shortfall on flagship road-fixing promise, requiring multiple terms and bipartisan deal in 2024. Recent focus on civil rights updates and tribal partnership.
summary.grade_rationale: Strong delivery on social policy (childcare, education, abortion access, labor rights) and some economic development wins. Chronic failure on marquee infrastructure promise damaged credibility despite eventual road funding passage. Limited data on 2025 promise tracking.

promises.normalized: Fix Michigan roads with long-term bipartisan funding solution
Expand free pre-K and tuition-free community college access statewide
Expand affordable childcare access to 150,000 additional families
Protect abortion access rights and modernize civil rights protections

data_gaps: Specific 2025 State of the State promise tracking beyond road funding mention
Quantified metrics on EV battery plant deal outcomes and job creation
Detailed breakdown of right-to-work repeal implementation status
Complete list of 14 promises from 2025 address for comprehensive grading
Election margin data from 2022 gubernatorial race
Timeline and attribution for tribal partnership initiatives
Status of civil rights law updates mentioned in sources
SOAR Fund performance metrics and actual investment results
